episodes.title: Growing Up In A Sex Cult with Inka Winter
episodes.description:
Adult filmmaker Inka Winter shares her firsthand experience of growing up in a controversial European commune (aka, cult) in the 1970s. The cult was disguised as a "liberated socialist experiment", but the reality was far from utopia.

There wasn’t any property. Clothes, toys, everything was communal. Even relationships were treated as property to be abolished. Attachment was seen as a negative—even for kids. Inka Winter was separated from parental figures whenever she got “too attached.”

The leader, Otto Mühl, called it his “living art.” But the real art was the manipulation.

However, don’t be misled. This is not the often misquoted archetype story of someone in the industry. Rather than focusing on trauma, this conversation centers on Inka's unique insights, resilience, and clear-eyed look at the systems that shaped her. Inka has taken her childhood and grown from the mud, developing a fascinating perspective on community, control, and the search for personal autonomy.

Yes, this story is devastating. But this story does not end here. Make sure you tune into the full episode to hear how Inka rose out of this cult upbringing!

––
